business.title: Quote of the day by Edwin Lefevre: "It is not the certainty of disaster ahead but the uncertainty of better days to come that keeps the investor from buying"
business.summary: Financial wisdom highlights that markets often reward those who act amidst uncertainty. Waiting for absolute clarity during downturns can lead to missed opportunities as stock prices recover. History shows strong rallies often begin with weak sentiment. Successful investing hinges on discipline, focusing on long-term fundamentals rather than short-term fears. Uncertainty, therefore, presents a chance to acquire quality assets at good prices for superior returns.
